commit_msg: chore: add ytpipeline placeholder for future ffmpeg video output
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Future He Left Empty
subtitle: He marked the spot where the video would go. Then he walked away.
when_where: Still 9:05 AM on main, same hour as the last confession. But the coffee was cold now. The placeholder sat in the code like a promise he had no intention of keeping—a door frame with no door, waiting for ffmpeg to walk through.
who_whom: Omkar and the future version of himself. A chore, a placeholder, a note that said 'video output coming'—but it never came. The code would sit there, untouched, a monument to the work he'd never finish. Every developer who read it would know: he dreamed bigger than he could deliver.
what_why: He planted a flag for ffmpeg video output and left it there like a grave marker. No implementation, no flesh, just a skeleton of intention. The grind montage was real—three days, no sleep, just code—but this placeholder was the confession that even he knew he wouldn't survive to finish it. He was already planning his exit, already writing the epitaph for work he hadn't done.
verdict: The cruelest crime isn't the code you write; it's the code you leave as a promise to a version of yourself you'll never be.
